title: rosa arvensis subsp. gallicoides
intro: Rosa arvensis subsp. gallicoides, also known as the field rose or wild rose, is a subspecies of the Rosa arvensis plant that is native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Rosa arvensis subsp. gallicoides include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and skin conditions.
